text: The surgeon inserts an optical trocar using the modified Scandinavian technique at the Inter-Anglican crease, followed by placing a 5mm working port in the suprapubic region.
The surgeon uses a gut-friendly grasp to locate the appendix, following the tinea coli to reach its base. The appendix is observed lying retrocecally.
The surgeon identifies the ureter as a white structure running along the pelvic side wall, with the ovarian vessels located above it. The appendix appears acutely inflamed.

The surgeon inserts a 5mm working port in the left mid-clavicular line, positioned midway between the optical port and the suprapubic port. The surgeon holds a ligature Maryland in the left hand and a grasping instrument in the right hand.

The surgeon uses an energy device, such as a harmonic or ligature, held in the left hand through the suprapubic port to dissect the mesoappendix along the long axis of the appendix. The dissection is performed carefully to ensure no fatty tissue or remnants of the mesoappendix remain attached, facilitating the removal of a slim appendix through a five mm port.
The surgeon prepares to ligate the base of the appendix using a LigaClip, having replaced the suprapubic working port with a Canon instrument.
And here we are done with this procedure. Thank you.
